qa_pairs.question: What persistent identifier is used for researchers?
Which persistent identifier is designated for data?
What is the recommended persistent identifier for research organizations?
Which persistent identifier should be used for research projects?
When is UUID suitable as a persistent identifier?
qa_pairs.answer: UUID (Universally unique identifier, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier) is suitable for metadata and other situations where a DOI is not appropriate.

qa_pairs.question: What persistent identifier is used for physical samples?
Can you list some persistent identifiers and their uses?
qa_pairs.answer: Persistent identifiers include ORCID (https://orcid.org/) for researchers, DOI (https://www.doi.org/) for data, ROR (https://ror.org/) for research organizations, RAiD (https://www.raid.org.au/) for research projects, UUID (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier) for metadata or when a DOI is not suitable, and IGSN (https://www.igsn.org/) for physical samples.
